LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE MATTER WAS CALLED AT 11:10 A.M. DUE TO THE REQUEST OF PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF NANTONG JOYFUL FASHION CO., LTD'S APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT IS GRANTED AS TO DEFENDANTS GEORGIOU STUDIO, INC. AND GEORGE GEORGIOU. PER 484.090, THE COURT FINDS THAT (1) THE CLAIM UPON WHICH THE ATTACHMENT IS BASED IS ONE UPON WHICH AN ATTACHMENT MAY BE ISSUED, (2) PLAINTIFFS HAVE ESTABLISHED THE PROBABLE VALIDITY OF THE CLAIM UPON WHICH THE ATTACHMENT IS BASED, (3) THE ATTACHMENT IS NOT SOUGHT FOR A PURPOSE OTHER THAN THE RECOVERY ON THE CLAIM UPON WHICH THE ATTACHMENT IS BASED, AND (4) THE AMOUNT TO BE SECURED BY THE ATTACHMENT IS GREATER THAN ZERO. PLAINTIFFS SHALL POST AN UNDERTAKING IN THE AMOUNT OF $10,000 PURSUANT TO CCP 489.020. DEFENDANTS' OBJECTION TO THE AMOUNT OF UNDERTAKING IS OVERRULED. (SEE NORTH HOLLYWOOD MARBLE CO. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1984) 157 CAL.APP.3D 683, 690-91, HOLDING A PLAINTIFF SHOULD NOT BE REQUIRED TO POST A "MASSIVE UNDERTAKING WHENEVER THE DEFENDANT CAN DEMONSTRATE THAT IT IS IN SUCH A PRECARIOUS FINANCIAL CONDITION THAT A LAWFUL ATTACHMENT WOULD RESULT IN PROBABLE DAMAGE OR DESTRUCTION OF ITS ENTIRE BUSINESS.") THE REQUEST FOR CONTINUANCE IS DENIED. 2 ORDERS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summ Adj; Evidence
Off Calendar Nov-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. X. YOUNG LAI, TEL. NO. (408)228-3995 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. JACK B. BURSTEIN OF THE LAW OFFICES OF SMITH & BURSTEIN, TEL. NO. (707)643-8405 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS NANTONG JOYFUL FASHION CO., LTD, BEIJING MENGYAZI GARMENTS CO., LTD AND BEIJING YANDI GARMENTS CO., LTD'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or alternatively Summary Adjudication - The Motion is granted in part and denied in part. Summary adjudication is granted in favor of Plaintiff Nantong Joyful Fashion ("NJF") on its cause of action for breach of contract. Plaintiff met its burden of showing that Defendant agreed to the terms of the "Sales Contract," and that Defendant failed to pay the amount owed. Defendant failed to rebut that showing because evidence of a prior agreement between the parties does not negate the terms agreed to by the parties in the sales contract. Summary adjudication is denied for Plaintiffs Beijing Mengyazi Garments Co., Ltd. and Beijing Yandi Garments Co., Ltd. (collectively "BMYG") for breach of contract. BMYG's evidence indicates that the parties agreed to payment on a consignment basis, yet BMYG failed to produce any evidence that all of the merchandise received by Defendants was actually sold. Absent this evidence, BMYG has failed to establish what amount was due from Defendants pursuant to the agreement. Summary adjudication is denied as moot to NJF for its cause of action for common counts as NJF has shown that it is entitled to judgment pursuant to breach of express contract. Judgment is denied as to BMYG because BMYG has failed to establish that no triable issues exist as to the amount due under the contract. For this same lack of evidence, judgment is also denied for conversion. Judgment is granted in favor of Plaintiffs as to all of Defendants' affirmative defenses, as none of these defenses are available to Defendants. (See UMF Issue 8, #2; UMF Issue 9, #1.) Judgment is granted in favor of NJF as to all causes of action in the Cross-Complaint. The evidence indicates that the parties entered a purchase and sale agreement and Defendants have presented no evidence raising a triable issue as to any causes of action against NJF. Judgment is denied as to BMYG on all causes of action in the Cross-Complaint because triable issues exist as to whether the parties were operating as a partnership or joint venture. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

Plaintiff Nantong Joyful Fashion Co., Ltd'S Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance
Off Calendar Sep-25-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. X. YOUNG LAI, TEL. NO. (408) 228-3995 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JACK B. BURSTEIN OF THE LAW OFFICES OF SMITH & BURSTEIN, TEL. NO. (707) 643-8405 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS CALLED AT 11:10 A.M. DUE TO THE REQUEST OF PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Nantong Joyful Fashion Co., Ltd's Application for Right to Attach Order and Order for Issuance of Writ of Attachment is granted as to Defendants Georgiou Studio, Inc. and George Georgiou. Per 484.090, the Court finds that (1) the claim upon which the attachment is based is one upon which an attachment may be issued, (2) Plaintiffs have established the probable validity of the claim upon which the attachment is based, (3) the attachment is not sought for a purpose other than the recovery on the claim upon which the attachment is based, and (4) the amount to be secured by the attachment is greater than zero. Plaintiffs shall post an undertaking in the amount of $10,000 pursuant to CCP 489.020. Defendants' objection to the amount of undertaking is overruled. (See North Hollywood Marble Co. v. Superior Court (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 683, 690-91, holding a plaintiff should not be required to post a "massive undertaking whenever the defendant can demonstrate that it is in such a precarious financial condition that a lawful attachment would result in probable damage or destruction of its entire business.") The Request for continuance is denied. 2 ORDERS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/LCN)
